body_txt: Cousin Oliver is a kid added to the show's roster, usually in an attempt to liven up an aging cast with a character they hope younger demographics can relate to. Depending on how deft the writers are at adding him, Cousin Oliver can become a fan favorite Ensemble Dark Horse or The Scrappy ( or worse ). A Cousin Oliver can disrupt the dynamic of the show, especially when his only role is to be cute and innocently wisecrack; writers can go past "sweetly precocious" and tumble right into "overbearing and annoying", especially to fans who care more about established characters. Sometimes Cousin Oliver is the logical result of a character's season-long pregnancy arc. Once the writers have exhausted the possibilities of new baby hijinks, the infant undergoes Soap Opera Rapid Aging Syndrome — or even a Plot-Relevant Age-Up if the setting allows for it — so they can become a regular member of the cast. Compare Kid-Appeal Character , who is generally there from the beginning. If the addition is a literal cousin, see also Nephewism . The character will often be a Token Houseguest . Frequently, the character is Totally Radical . Often a form of Jumping the Shark . If there's actually an in-series point to the character, compare Cain and Abel and Seth .
